claims.claim_text: The release target defines a 64-entry L1/L2 vaBench coverage plan.
claims.safe_wording: The current release package defines 64 planned L1/L2 entries; this is a coverage target, not a final scored benchmark result.
unsafe_wording: vaBench is fully certified because it has 64 rows.
The 64-entry table alone proves benchmark correctness.

claims.claim_text: All release entries have materialized source-task assets and pass static checks.
claims.safe_wording: The release has 64 materialized entries for the 64-entry plan and 219 static-certified forms with zero asset issues.
unsafe_wording: Static certification proves EVAS/Spectre behavioral parity.
Asset materialization means the task is scored.

claims.claim_text: The imported EVAS/Spectre evidence subset has no EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL mismatch.
claims.safe_wording: On the imported certified subset (217 forms), EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL count is 0.
unsafe_wording: Imported parity evidence enables benchmark scores by itself.
Zero EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL proves speedup or model baseline claims.
notes: This is an imported-evidence subset claim only.

claims.claim_text: The full release package is EVAS/Spectre certified.
claims.safe_wording: This claim is blocked until every release form has current EVAS/Spectre evidence with zero dual failures and zero EVAS PASS / Spectre FAIL mismatches.
unsafe_wording: vaBench release is fully EVAS/Spectre certified.
All release tasks pass Spectre and EVAS.
required_before_allowed: complete fresh rerun for 2 queued forms (includes 0 historical source-equivalence blockers)
bridge diagnostics must report a ready profile
fresh dual rerun summary must import successfully

claims.claim_text: The release benchmark has an enabled score denominator.
claims.safe_wording: The release benchmark score denominator is enabled for 51 certified content-denominator entries and 184 forms.
unsafe_wording: Current baseline percentages are release benchmark scores.
The 64 planned entries are already the scored denominator.

claims.claim_text: EVAS has measured same-slice speed/debug advantages over Spectre on the release package.
claims.safe_wording: Speed/debug has subset timing evidence, but release-wide speedup remains blocked until the dedicated artifact marks the speed claim allowed.
unsafe_wording: EVAS debug advantage is proven by the current release artifacts.
required_before_allowed: Run same-slice EVAS/Spectre timing for every scored release form, or state a narrower subset-only claim.
Stratify or fix EVAS slow outliers before claiming aggregate speedup on the release package.
Keep per-row EVAS and Spectre wall-clock timings from run_gold_dual_suite timing fields.
Keep Spectre-reported runtime from spectre.out alongside wrapper wall-clock timings.
Report machine/bridge/Cadence configuration with the timing artifact.
Do not compute speedup from historical main120 summaries because they lack same-slice runtime metadata.

claims.claim_text: Model baselines are scored on the clean vaBench release package.
claims.safe_wording: Model baseline reporting is pending until baseline runs report against the enabled score denominator.
unsafe_wording: The current unscored release rows support model baseline results.
Historical candidate runs are release benchmark baselines.
required_before_allowed: baseline runs must report against counted release entries/forms only

claims.claim_text: L0 EVAS/Spectre conformance cases are kept outside the L1/L2 benchmark denominator.
claims.safe_wording: L0 conformance has 4 cases and contributes 0 entries to benchmark coverage.
unsafe_wording: L0 conformance cases increase the 64-entry benchmark size.
Conformance regressions are scored release tasks.

claims.claim_text: The clean vaBench release package is complete.
claims.safe_wording: The package structure and source assets are ready, but full completion remains blocked by fresh dual rerun, scoring, speed, and baseline gates.
required_before_allowed: external blocker report active: 0 blocked, 1 pending

claim_policy: Allowed claims may be used only with their safe_wording scope.
Blocked claims must not appear as paper conclusions, abstract claims, figure captions, or benchmark result text.
Partial imported evidence must not be phrased as full-release certification.
Score, speed, and baseline claims require an enabled denominator and fresh release evidence.
